title: Would You Like A Robot Companion?

blanks.sentence: Robotics - the _____ robots
These machines assist with household chores and _____
Not everyone is comfortable with having _____ in their homes
Despite this, AI enables robots to form _____ with people
Designer: Grace Brown, an engineer from _____
During the pandemic, she experienced no _____ for five months
Built Abi to offer _____ to those feeling isolated
Now manufactures robots for _____ through her company Andromeda
Target users: senior citizens in _____ with few visitors
Personality traits: _____, inquisitive and curious
Visitors are often _____ by her behaviour
Appears to possess _____ – acts independently
Can perform entertaining actions like blowing _____
Height: approximately _____ tall
Similar in size to a _____
Appearance includes _____ and large eyes
Eyes are highly _____, revealing emotions
Design inspired by animated characters from _____
Regular conversations with Abi bring _____ and a sense of community to residents
Regular conversations with Abi bring joy and _____ to residents

multipleChoice.question: What is Neil's attitude towards having a humanoid robot living with him?
options: A. He feels he already has enough people around him
B. He welcomes having a robot to help with chores
C. He is excited about the idea of a robot companion
D. He worries about the robot's safety and control

multipleChoice.question: According to the conversation, what is the intended purpose of building Abi, the robot?
options: A. To entertain children by performing tricks and games
B. To provide elderly people with company and reduce loneliness
C. To carry out household cleaning and maintenance tasks
D. To replace human caregivers in nursing homes completely

multipleChoice.question: In the transcript, what does the word "sassy" imply about Abi's personality?
options: A. She is shy and reserved when meeting people
B. She acts aggressively and unpredictably
C. She is bold, confident, and slightly cheeky
D. She is highly logical and unemotional

multipleChoice.question: What does it mean when Grace says Abi “has a mind of her own”?
options: A. Abi always follows human instructions perfectly
B. Abi behaves independently, beyond human control
C. Abi is programmed to obey all commands strictly
D. Abi does not show any personality or curiosity

multipleChoice.question: Why did Grace Brown choose to model Abi to look like a young child?
options: A. To make Abi appear approachable and endearing
B. To make Abi seem more powerful and intimidating
C. To minimize the robot's size for easier transportation
D. To ensure Abi blends in with adult users better

multipleChoice.question: Which of the following best expresses the effect Abi has on elderly residents?
options: A. Abi replaces the need for family visits
B. Abi causes confusion and fear among residents
C. Abi brings joy and a feeling of community
D. Abi increases physical activity among the elderly

multipleChoice.question: What contrast does the conversation make between Abi and robots in sci-fi movies?
options: A. Abi is violent and unpredictable like movie robots
B. Abi is designed for military use rather than companionship
C. Abi is friendly and approachable, unlike typical sci-fi robots
D. Abi is less advanced technologically than fictional robots

multipleChoice.question: What reason does Grace give for Abi having large, expressive eyes?
options: A. To allow her to see better in dark places
B. To show emotions and make her feel more relatable
C. To give her a scary appearance to protect children
D. To focus more on surveillance and monitoring tasks

multipleChoice.question: What can be inferred about people's reaction when they first meet Abi?
options: A. They immediately distrust her technology
B. They expect a robot to lack any personality
C. They find her dull and uninteresting at first
D. They are usually surprised by her confident attitude

multipleChoice.question: Why is Abi described as having “a splash of colour” in her design?
options: A. To hide mechanical parts from people's view
B. To imitate the colour of human skin tones
C. To camouflage her in care home environments
D. To make her visually attractive and less dull
